description: Inter-group bonds, diplomatic agreements, and cooperative pacts.
factions: Alliance
Multiple powers pledging mutual aid, their fates bound together against a common threat.
Coalition
Rival groups setting aside grudges for a single urgent cause, knowing the truce will not last.
Confederation
Independent groups cooperating loosely under a shared banner, each jealously guarding its own sovereignty.
Federation
Member states yielding some freedom to a central authority while clinging to local autonomy.
Bloc
Nations or factions locked arm in arm to present a unified front, daring outsiders to test their solidarity.
Axis
A dominant partnership anchoring a web of lesser allies and pulling others into its orbit.
Front
Disparate groups merged under one name to project strength, masking their internal fractures.
Confederacy
Sovereign states banded together for survival, each contributing on its own terms and resenting outside orders.
Triumvirate
Three powerful figures sharing rule but each scheming to outmaneuver the other two.
League
Cities or states pledging defense and trade to one another, rising or falling as one.
Compact
Signatories sworn to a binding agreement, their honor staked on every clause.
Pact
A solemn vow between parties, sealed by blood, magic, or mutual desperation.
Treaty
Formal terms hammered out on parchment between nations, the ink barely restraining old hostilities.
Accord
Former adversaries negotiating a fragile peace, each watching for the first sign of betrayal.
Concordat
A sacred agreement between secular and religious powers, backed by faith and political necessity alike.
Quorum
The minimum assembly needed to act, where every absent seat shifts the balance of power.
Convention
Delegates gathering to forge agreements, trading concessions behind closed doors before any public vote.
Summit
The most powerful leaders meeting face to face, where a handshake can avert war or ignite one.
Entente
An unwritten understanding between powers, bound by goodwill rather than parchment -- and easily broken.
